rag_rules: Repair validator diagnostics by adding missing annex syntax or fixing type references
Preserve existing architecture when repairing AGREE syntax errors
generic_rules: Use only valid AGREE scalar types (int, real, bool) in const and eq declarations
Place assumptions and guarantees in the correct scope: component type annex for type-level contracts, implementation annex for implementation-level bindings
Use visible AADL features or eq helpers declared in the same annex for guarantees
Remove invalid guarantees instead of adding architecture to fix unresolved references
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Implementation-level bindings should be placed in implementation annexes after subcomponents and connections
anti_patterns: Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Do not place implementation-level references in component type annexes
Do not use invalid AGREE type names like 'integer' or 'boolean'
